regime_justification: USDJPY near 159, BOJ Deputy Governor Himino confirmed continued rate-hike intent but avoided signaling timing, JGB yields near 30-year highs, oil easing on US-Iran deal hope, vol low but carry spread intact.

domino_stage.label: Pressure Building
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY 158.91, 0.18 yen below 160 intervention watch line; JGB 10Y at 2.71% (near 30-yr highs); BOJ signaling more hikes but cautious on timing; yen/bbl elevated despite oil pullback; risk assets bid.

1w.label: Unlikely
1w.driver: USDJPY stable mid-158s, oil down 6% on deal optimism, BOJ next meeting June 15-16 (3 weeks out), vol subdued, no imminent catalyst.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Applied unwind prior; would otherwise grade D. BOJ meeting June 15-16 inside window; Himino rhetoric keeps hike risk live; USDJPY one headline from 160.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: BOJ June 15-16 meeting high-stakes; market pricing ~40% hike by Dec; Iran deal fragility could re-spike oil; spread still 187 bps but vol compression fragile.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Post-BOJ clarity expected to resolve near-term tension; if no hike and oil stays low, carry extends; if hike, controlled normalization path likely.

bottom_line: Stage 1: Pressure building but contained. USDJPY 158.91 (18 pips from 160 watch line), JGB 10Y 2.71% (30-yr highs), US 10Y 4.56%, spread 187 bps. Oil -6% on US-Iran deal hope eases import cost pressure but ¥/bbl still elevated at 14,524. BOJ Deputy Governor Himino confirmed rate-hike intent Monday but gave no timing, keeping June 15-16 meeting live. VIX 16.75, MOVE 78.4—vol calm. Nikkei +2.87% to record 65,158. BTC $76,643. Grades: 1w D, 2w C (prior applied), 3w C (BOJ meeting), 4w D. Watch: BOJ June decision, oil whipsaw if Iran talks fail, USDJPY break above 159.50.

Low; 52w range 13.38–35.30; equity vol subdued despite geopolitical noise
Below 80 = calm bond vol; 52w high 140; rates vol compressed despite JGB near highs

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 158.91, 18 pips below 160. Last intervention ~April 30 & May 6 (reports). Katayama rhetoric active; timing unpredictable.
Next meeting June 15-16. Himino (Deputy Gov) confirmed hike intent Monday but 'timing depends on ME conflict impact.' April minutes showed hawkish minority.
2.71% today, pulled back from 2.78% Friday (30-yr high). Fiscal concerns + BOJ taper = upside risk if oil re-spikes or BOJ dovish surprise.
78.4 today, calm. Would need sharp rates repricing or funding stress to trigger.
Oil -6% Monday on deal hope but US-Iran talks fragile. Trump warned 'fresh attacks if talks fail.' Hormuz closure risk live; ¥/bbl 14,524 vs ~10k pre-conflict.
Data unavailable today. Historical pattern: flips precede intervention. Monitor next week.

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY steady 158.91 (+0.02%), Nikkei +2.87% to record 65,158, oil +0.45% to $91.39 after Monday's -6% plunge on US-Iran deal optimism. JGB 10Y pulled back to 2.71% (-7 bps) from Friday's 30-yr high of 2.78%. BOJ Deputy Governor Himino told parliament Monday Japan will 'continue to raise rates' but gave no timing, noting close monitoring of Middle East impact on economy/prices. No stage change from yesterday's yellow/Stage 1; pressure remains elevated but contained.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 187 bps (4.56% - 2.71%), widened +4 bps as JGB fell faster than UST. Carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (USDJPY 1M implied vol data sparse today). USDJPY 18 pips below 160 MOF intervention threshold—tight but stable. 5-day % change flat (~0%); 60-day mean ~158.5, so price neutral to slightly stretched. ¥/bbl crude 14,524 (vs ~10k pre-conflict), still elevated despite oil retreat. VIX 16.75, MOVE 78.4—both calm. Domino Stage 1: carry mechanics intact (spread 187 bps, vol low) but policy reaction brewing (BOJ June 15-16 meeting live, Himino rhetoric hawkish-ish) and energy tail risk persists (Iran talks fragile). Applied unwind prior to 2w grade: data genuinely mixed (BOJ meeting inside window, USDJPY near line, but vol/oil benign short-term); would otherwise grade D, but meeting catalyst and prior warrant C. 1w grade D: no imminent trigger. 3w C: BOJ meeting high-stakes. 4w D: post-decision clarity expected.
narrative.p3: BTC $76,643 (+1.16%), holding above $75k support despite $2.26B net ETF outflows over two weeks and rising UST yields. 24h volume $22B, healthy but not euphoric. BTC-DXY correlation data unavailable but macro context suggests risk-off undertow (tightening liquidity, real rates rising) offset by AI/risk-on Nikkei melt-up. Crypto sympathy to Japan equity surge muted—suggests BTC more macro-sensitive than Asia equity beta right now. $75k-$77k range consolidation; break below $75k would signal deeper trouble, break above $80k needs macro easing (Fed pivot or oil collapse). For positioning: BTC holding but not leading; watch $75k floor and ETF flow reversals as liquidity tells.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1w D → 2w C: BOJ June 15-16 meeting moves inside 2-week window by end of this week. Himino confirmed hike intent but no timing = binary risk. USDJPY 18 pips from 160; one headline (Iran, MOF jawbone, data surprise) flips grade. Oil benign near-term but deal fragility = tail risk. Applied prior as tiebreaker given setup ambiguity.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2w C → 3w C: BOJ meeting June 15-16 sits in week 3. Market pricing ~40% December hike (per FOMC minutes context) but BOJ April minutes showed hawkish minority pressing for 'no long intervals' between hikes. If Iran talks collapse, oil re-spikes, yen import cost pressure re-emerges, BOJ forced hawkish. If deal holds and BOJ stands pat, relief rally but carry fragility persists (JGB at 30-yr highs, fiscal concerns). Both paths keep C.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3w C → 4w D: Post-BOJ decision (June 15-16) clarity expected to resolve near-term binary. If hike, controlled normalization narrative likely (Himino: 'real rates still extremely low'); USDJPY dips but doesn't crash. If hold + dovish, carry extends, spreads widen, unwind risk deferred. Either way, immediate catalyst resolved. Oil trajectory post-Iran deal (or collapse) also clearer by week 4. Grade steps down to D unless June decision surprises hawkish AND spreads compress fast.

watch_list: BOJ June 15-16 meeting decision & Ueda press conference tone—hike probability and forward guidance on pace
USDJPY break above 159.50 or below 158.00—former raises MOF intervention risk, latter signals carry unwind beginning
US-Iran deal progress—Hormuz reopening vs talks collapse; oil whipsaw would re-price ¥/bbl and BOJ inflation path
JGB 10Y above 2.80%—would mark new 30-yr high and test fiscal sustainability narrative; watch BOJ taper pace
MOVE index above 85 or VIX above 18—would signal rates/equity vol regime shift and potential carry liquidation trigger
